paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture within a decoder-only Transformer framework, enabling efficient scaling of model capacity while reducing computational costs through sparse activation
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Webpages (143B tokens), Wikipedia (3B), Conversations (174B), Forums (247B), Books (390B), News (650B)
raw_extraction.application: Natural Language Understanding (NLU) and Natural Language Generation (NLG) tasks, including zero-, one-, and few-shot learning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models with reduced compute requirements for large-scale training/inference
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Uses a sparsely-activated MoE architecture with 64 experts per layer, reducing compute intensity while maintaining performance comparable to dense baselines at similar parameter scales
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Webpages (143B tokens), Wikipedia (3B), Conversations (174B), Forums (247B), Books (390B), News (650B)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models with reduced compute requirements for large-scale training/inference
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces a medium-sized MoE model with 32 experts per layer, balancing performance and computational efficiency
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Webpages (143B tokens), Wikipedia (3B), Conversations (174B), Forums (247B), Books (390B), News (650B)
raw_extraction.application: NLU/NLG tasks with optimized resource usage
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models with reduced compute requirements for large-scale training/inference
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: A large MoE model with 64 experts per layer, demonstrating superior performance on NLU/NLG tasks with fewer compute resources compared to dense alternatives
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Webpages (143B tokens), Wikipedia (3B), Conversations (174B), Forums (247B), Books (390B), News (650B)
raw_extraction.application: Advanced NLU/NLG tasks requiring strong generalization capabilities
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models with reduced compute requirements for large-scale training/inference
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: An enhanced MoE model with 64 experts per layer, offering improved performance on complex NLP tasks with substantial parameter savings
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Webpages (143B tokens), Wikipedia (3B), Conversations (174B), Forums (247B), Books (390B), News (650B)
raw_extraction.application: High-capacity NLU/NLG applications needing robust generalization
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of language models with reduced compute requirements for large-scale training/inference
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces the Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture to efficiently scale language models while maintaining computational efficiency through sparse activation patterns. By partitioning computation across many lightweight experts, GLaM achieves better predictive performance than dense counterparts with similar FLOPs
raw_extraction.application: General language understanding, question answering, reasoning tasks
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of transformer-based language models with limited compute resources
raw_extraction.extension: Uses 2D sharding algorithm to partition weights and activations across devices, allowing identical computation graphs for different MoE layers without redundancy
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Same architecture as base model but increases the number of experts per layer from 1 to 64, improving model capacity and flexibility while maintaining computational efficiency
raw_extraction.application: General language understanding, question answering, reasoning tasks
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of transformer-based language models with limited compute resources
raw_extraction.extension: Increased expert count enables more specialized computations per layer while preserving sparsity benefits from the original MoE design
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Extends the MoE architecture further by increasing experts per layer to 256, demonstrating exponential growth in model capacity while maintaining computational efficiency through sparse activation patterns
raw_extraction.application: General language understanding, question answering, reasoning tasks
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of transformer-based language models with limited compute resources
raw_extraction.extension: Highest expert density tested, requiring careful weight partitioning across devices to avoid redundancy while maximizing model performance
raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces **mixture-of-experts (MoE)** layers to improve efficiency while scaling to massive sizes (~64B parameters), reducing computational cost by dynamically routing tokens across sparse expert networks during training/inference
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Filtered/unfiltered large-scale text corpora (details implied by training tokens)
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language understanding and generation, including reasoning, question answering, and natural language processing tasks

raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficiently scaling transformer-based language models with high parameter counts

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces **mixture-of-experts (MoE)** layers to improve efficiency while scaling to massive sizes (~64B parameters), reducing computational cost by dynamically routing tokens across sparse expert networks during training/inference
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language understanding and generation, including reasoning, question answering, and natural language processing tasks

raw_extraction.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces **mixture-of-experts (MoE)** layers alongside a larger dense baseline model (137B parameters) to compare efficiency improvements. Demonstrates superior performance-per-compute trade-offs
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language understanding and generation, including reasoning, question answering, and natural language processing tasks
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficiently scaling transformer-based language models with high parameter counts
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with dense layers (baseline)
